## Page 182

### [p182_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p182_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p182_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p182_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (42nd Street Times Square)
### [p182_c0005] 1.08 – MR 317 | 42nd Street / Times Square Station
[p182_c0006] Summary: 42nd Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 32" minimum pinch point requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met at the south end of the platform as the remaining width would be 27" (see figure 1).
### [p182_c0007] Description
[p182_c0008] The 42nd Street Station is a below-grade station consisting two center / island platforms. The platforms are approximately 21'-2" wide throughout. At the southern end of the northbound platform there are 42" between the column and the platform edge. The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width to 27" or less* which would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p182_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p182_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant ADA condition 42nd Street Station_
[p182_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p182_c0012] _Page 15 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 183

### [p183_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p183_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p183_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p183_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (34 th Street Penn Station)
### [p183_c0005] 1.09 – MR 318 | 34 th Street Penn Station
[p183_c0006] Summary: 34 th Street Penn Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the southbound platform as the existing width is 5'-0" (see figure 1).
### [p183_c0007] Description
[p183_c0008] 34 th Street Station is a below-grade station consisting of both side and center / island platform. The express 2 and 3 trains utilize the center / island platform. The platform is approximately 24'-8" wide throughout, narrowing to 5'-0" at the south end of the southbound platform and 5'-3" at the northbound platform. Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress via emergency exit doors with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
> [p183_c0009] A photograph of a subway platform at 34th Street Penn Station. A yellow 3D box labeled 'PSD' is superimposed on the platform, representing a platform screen door. Red dimension lines are drawn on the floor, indicating widths of 30 inches, 27 inches, and 15 inches. The 15-inch width is the narrowest section of the platform, which is the area of concern for egress conditions.
[p183_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition 34 th Street Station_
[p183_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p183_c0012] _Page 16 of 88 March 11, 2019_

In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36” minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met at south end of the southbound platform as the remaining width would be 33” (see figure 1).
### [p184_c0007] Description
[p184_c0008] The 28th Street Station is a below-grade station with two platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms are approximately 11'-10". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width at the south end of the southbound platform below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 33" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.

In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the northbound platform as the existing width is 5'-10" (see figure 1).

[p185_c0009] Platform width at the ends of the northbound & southbound platform are 5'-10" or 70". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.

In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the northbound platform as the existing width is 5'-0" (see figure 1).
### [p187_c0007] Description

## Page 188

### [p188_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p188_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p188_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p188_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Christopher Street Station)
### [p188_c0005] 1.14 – MR 323 | Christopher Street Station
[p188_c0006] Summary: Christopher Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to lack of available space for the PSD equipment room.
### [p188_c0007] Description
[p188_c0008] Christopher Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Platform widths vary from 10'-4" to 11'-8". There is a single row of columns on each platform.
[p188_c0009] Due to the limited width of the existing platforms, there is no available space for the equipment room. Figure 2 below shows the minimum width required (12'-11") for construction of a PSD equipment room on a station platform. Figure 1, below, demonstrates the lack of available space within the southbound control area. The northbound control area is similar.
[p188_c0010] _Figure 1 – Congested/Narrow Station Plan Christopher Street Station_
[p188_c0011] _Figure 2 – Diagram demonstrating minimum platform width dimensions (A Division train shown; B Division requires same dimension)_
[p188_c0012] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p188_c0013] _Page 21 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 189

### [p189_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p189_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p189_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations
### [p189_c0004] (Houston Street Station)
### [p189_c0005] 1.15 – MR 324 | Houston Street Station
[p189_c0006] Summary: Houston Street is feasible for both APGs and PSDs. Platform edge reconstruction may be required to support the requirements of an APG system (see Appendix B). Existing power is adequate.
### [p189_c0007] Description
[p189_c0008] Houston Street Station is a below ground station with two side platforms ( see Figure 1 ). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Columns are located throughout the length of the platforms along the platform edge. Column faces measure approximately 4'-0" from the platform edge. The platform widths are approximately 12-0" throughout. Ceiling heights measure no less than 7'-6" throughout.
[p189_c0009] Full Height PSDs: Full height PSDs will require lateral structural bracing to the station ceiling as well as reconfiguration of existing ceiling-mounted systems to address edge-of-platform requirements of lighting, entrapment prevention sensors, CCTV, and standard NYCT wayfinding signage.
[p189_c0010] Half Height PSDs (aka APGs): This system is less likely to affect existing lighting and other systems on the ceiling. Depending on the half height PSD design, sensors may be ceiling-mounted or mounted on the APG unit itself. In any case, there will be a CCTV camera over each motorized sliding door which will need to be in coordination with existing or replacement lighting.
### [p189_c0011] Equipment Room
[p189_c0012] The equipment room can be located at the southbound control area of the station ( see Figure 1, Figure 2 ). The proposed room dimensions are 27'-6" x 7'-0".
### [p189_c0013] Track Layout
[p189_c0014] Tracks are tangent. Therefore we are not expecting that gaps between the platform and train will exacerbate the gap between the train doors and the PSDs. However, per NYCT standards, the Limiting Line of Line Equipment (LLE) would necessitate the placement of PSDs sufficiently distant from the train doors to create gaps that would have to be addressed by entrapment prevention measures.
### [p189_c0015] Platform Edge Condition
[p189_c0016] The platform edges were reconstructed in the early 1990's. From our limited visual inspection and our knowledge of repair strategies used in station rehabilitations over the last thirty years, structural work would at a minimum be required for the installation of an APG system.
[p189_c0017] _Page 22 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 192

### [p192_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p192_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p192_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Houston Street Station)
### [p192_c0004] Platform obstructions within 5' of edge:
- [p192_c0005] • Columns
[p192_c0006] Please note that in the ADA boarding zones, existing columns obstruct the 60" circle requirement discussed in the ADA summary in Appendix A. The installation of a PSD system would not further exacerbate these conditions.
### [p192_c0007] Lighting:
[p192_c0008] Existing lighting: Throughout both platforms there are linear florescent fixtures mounted parallel to the platform edge. Depending on the specific APG/PSD design used, there could be no or minimal alterations to the existing lighting configuration
### [p192_c0009] Power:
[p192_c0010] This station has adequate electrical capacity to support the implementation of an APG/PSD system. We do not consider a lack of adequate existing power to be a determining factor of future feasibility. If in the future, a PSD/APG project is to be implemented at this station, and it is determined at that time that an upgrade in power service to the station is required, it would simply mean an increased cost to the project. Below in Table 1 & Table 2 please see the Power Capacity Analysis for this station.
[p192_c0011] _Station Power Capacity Analysis (normal service)_
[p192_c0012]

```text
Station Name Houston Street Varick St. Peak Demand Load from ConEd Report, Last 20 Months, (kW) 40.8 Apparent Power (kVA) 51.0 Station Peak Demand Load, Max Current, (A) 141.7 Maximum Amount of Doors 60.0 PSD Total Load Including All Miscellaneous Loads, (A) 158.1 Total Load (Station Peak + PSD), (A) 299 Station Service Power Capacity, (Main SB or SG Rating), (A) 800 Service Spare Capacity, (A) 501 Is Electrical Service Adequate? Yes Notes Service capacity data is based on observations of breaker schedule. Only partial one line diagram (for tunnel lighting) is available.
```
[p192_c0013] _Table 1. Normal Service Power Capacity Analysis_
[p192_c0014] _Page 25 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 193

### [p193_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p193_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p193_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Houston Street Station)
### [p193_c0004] Station Power Capacity Analysis (reserve service)
[p193_c0005]

```text
Station Name Houston Street Varick St. Peak Demand Load from ConEd Report, Last 20 Months, (kW) 10.4 Apparent Power (kVA) 13.0 Station Peak Demand Load, Max Current, (A) 36.0 Maximum Amount of Doors 60.0 PSD Total Load Including All Miscellaneous Loads, (A) 158.1 Total Load (Station Peak + PSD), (A) 194 Station Service Power Capacity, (Main SB or SG Rating), (A) 800 Service Spare Capacity, (A) 606 Is Electrical Service Adequate? Yes Notes Service capacity data is based on observations of breaker schedule. Only partial one line diagram (for tunnel lighting) is available.
```

### [p193_c0007] Historic Restrictions:
[p193_c0008] None
### [p193_c0009] Rough Order-of-Magnitude Cost Estimate:
[p193_c0010] The rough order-of-magnitude (ROM) cost estimate is based on a summary of anticipated costs developed through a review of field conditions, analysis of space constraints and existing documentation. It is not based upon an actual conceptual design. The basis of estimate assumptions are listed in the attached ROM estimate. The total cost for this station is estimated to be $27.2M to install APGs and $34.2M to install PSDs (See Appendix E)
[p193_c0011] _Page 26 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 194

### [p194_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p194_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p194_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Canal Street Station)
### [p194_c0004] 1.16 – MR 325 | Canal Street Station
[p194_c0005] Summary: Canal Street Station is feasible for both APGs and PSDs. Platform edge reconstruction may be required to support the requirements of an APG system (see Appendix B). Existing power is adequate.
### [p194_c0006] Description
[p194_c0007] Canal Street Station is a below ground station with two side platforms ( see Figure 1 ). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Columns are located throughout the length of the platforms along the platform edge. Column faces measure approximately 4'-0" from the platform edge. The platform widths are approximately 12-0" throughout. Ceiling heights measure no less than 7'-6" throughout.
[p194_c0008] Full Height PSDs: Full height PSDs will require lateral structural bracing to the station ceiling as well as reconfiguration of existing ceiling-mounted systems to address edge-of-platform requirements of lighting, entrapment prevention sensors, CCTV, and standard NYCT wayfinding signage.
[p194_c0009] Half Height PSDs (aka APGs): This system is less likely to affect existing lighting and other systems on the ceiling. Depending on the half height PSD design, sensors may be ceiling-mounted or mounted on the APG unit itself. In any case, there will be a CCTV camera over each motorized sliding door which will need to be in coordination with existing or replacement lighting.
### [p194_c0010] Equipment Room
[p194_c0011] The equipment room can be located at the abandoned passageway at the south end of the station ( see Figure 1, Figure 2 ). The proposed room dimensions are 27'-6" x 7'-0".
### [p194_c0012] Track Layout
[p194_c0013] Tracks are tangent. Therefore we are not expecting that gaps between the platform and train will exacerbate the gap between the train doors and the PSDs. However, per NYCT standards, the Limiting Line of Line Equipment (LLLE) would necessitate the placement of PSDs sufficiently distant from the train doors to create gaps that would have to be addressed by entrapment prevention measures.
### [p194_c0014] Platform Edge Condition
[p194_c0015] The platform edges were reconstructed in the early 1990's. From our limited visual inspection and our knowledge of repair strategies used in station rehabilitations of the last thirty years, structural work would only be required for the installation of an APG system.
[p194_c0016] _Page 27 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 195

A legend in the lower-left corner defines these symbols: a grey square for 'ADA 60" TURNING AREA' and a small circle for 'COLUMN'. The word 'PLATFORM' is written on the left and right sides of the diagram. The text 'PLATFORM PLAN' is located on the left side, and 'LEGEND' is centered below the diagram.
[p195_c0006] _Figure 1 – Overall Station Plan Canal Street Station_

## Page 197

### [p197_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p197_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p197_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Canal Street Station)
### [p197_c0004] Platform obstructions within 5' of edge:
- [p197_c0005] • Columns
[p197_c0006] Please note that in the ADA boarding zones, existing columns obstruct the 60" circle requirement discussed in the ADA summary in Appendix A. The installation of a PSD system would not further exacerbate these conditions.
### [p197_c0007] Lighting:
[p197_c0008] Existing lighting: Throughout both platforms there are linear florescent fixtures mounted parallel to the platform edge on the inside face of the columns. No lighting re-configuration will be required as a result of a PSD installation.
### [p197_c0009] Power:
[p197_c0010] The Normal EDR was not accessible at the time of survey. However, the electrical reserve service has adequate electrical capacity to support the implementation of an APG/PSD system. We do not consider a lack of adequate existing power to be a determining factor of future feasibility. If in the future, a PSD/APG project is to be implemented at this station, and it is determined at that time that an upgrade in power service to the station is required, it would simply mean an increased cost to the project. Below in Table 1 please see the Power Capacity Analysis for this station.
[p197_c0011] _Station Power Capacity Analysis (reserve service)_
[p197_c0012]

```text
Station Name Canal Street Peak Demand Load from ConEd Report, Last 20 Months, (kW) 37.2 Apparent Power (kVA) 46.5 Station Peak Demand Load, Max Current, (A) 130.0 Maximum Amount of Doors 60.0 PSD Total Load Including All Miscellaneous Loads, (A) 158.1 Total Load (Station Peak + PSD), (A) 288 Station Service Power Capacity, (Main SB or SG Rating), (A) 800 Service Spare Capacity, (A) 312 Is Electrical Service Adequate? Yes Notes Service capacity data is based on observations of breaker schedule. Only partial one line diagram (for tunnel lighting) is available. Also the above capacity is based on demand KW for Reserve power.
```
[p197_c0013] _Table 1. Reserve Service Power Capacity Analysis_
[p197_c0014] _Page 30 of 88 March 11, 2019_

### [p199_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Franklin Street Station)
### [p199_c0004] 1.17 – MR 326 | Franklin Street Station
[p199_c0005] Summary: Franklin Street is feasible for both APGs and PSDs. Platform edge reconstruction may be required to support the requirements of an APG system (see Appendix B). Existing power is adequate.
### [p199_c0006] Description
[p199_c0007] Franklin Street Station is a below ground station with two side platforms ( see Figure 1 ). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Columns are located throughout the length of the platforms along the platform edge. Column faces measure approximately 4'-0" from the platform edge. The platform widths are approximately 11-4" throughout. Ceiling heights measure no less than 7'-6" throughout.
[p199_c0008] Full Height PSDs: Full height PSDs will require lateral structural bracing to the station ceiling as well as reconfiguration of existing ceiling-mounted systems to address edge-of-platform requirements of lighting, entrapment prevention sensors, CCTV, and standard NYCT wayfinding signage.
[p199_c0009] Half Height PSDs (aka APGs): This system is less likely to affect existing lighting and other systems on the ceiling. Depending on the half height PSD design, sensors may be ceiling-mounted or mounted on the APG unit itself. In any case, there will be a CCTV camera over each motorized sliding door which will need to be in coordination with existing or replacement lighting.
### [p199_c0010] Equipment Room
[p199_c0011] The equipment room can be located at the northbound control area of the station ( see Figure 1, Figure 2 ). The proposed room dimensions are 27'-6" x 7'-0".
### [p199_c0012] Track Layout
[p199_c0013] Tracks are tangent. Therefore we are not expecting that gaps between the platform and train will exacerbate the gap between the train doors and the PSDs. However, per NYCT standards, the Limiting Line of Line Equipment (LLE) would necessitate the placement of PSDs sufficiently distant from the train doors to create gaps that would have to be addressed by entrapment prevention measures.
### [p199_c0014] Platform Edge Condition
[p199_c0015] The platform edges were reconstructed in the early 1990's. From our limited visual inspection and our knowledge of repair strategies used in station rehabilitations of the last thirty years, structural work would only be required for the installation of an APG system.
[p199_c0016] _Page 32 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 204

### [p204_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p204_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p204_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p204_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Chambers Street Station)
### [p204_c0005] 1.18 – MR 327 | Chambers Street Station
[p204_c0006] Summary: Chambers Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 31" (see figure 1).
### [p204_c0007] Description
[p204_c0008] The Chambers Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight center / island platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms is approximately 17'-2". The corridor width at the southern end of the southbound platform is 46". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 31" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p204_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p204_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Chambers Street Station_
[p204_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p204_c0012] _Page 37 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 205

### [p205_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p205_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p205_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p205_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Park Place Station)
### [p205_c0005] 1.19 – MR 331 | Park Place Station
[p205_c0006] Summary: Park Place Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 32" minimum pinch point requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met at the escalator as the remaining width would be 23" (see figure 1).
### [p205_c0007] Description
[p205_c0008] The Park Place Station is a below-grade station with one straight center/island platform. The platform structure is cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platform is approximately 17'-10". At the columns on either side of the escalator, there is 38" clearance. The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 23" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p205_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p205_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Park Place Station_
[p205_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p205_c0012] _Page 38 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 206

### [p206_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p206_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p206_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p206_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Fulton Street Station)
### [p206_c0005] 1.20 – MR 332 | Fulton St. Station
[p206_c0006] Summary: Fulton St. Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36” minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 31” (see figure 1).
### [p206_c0007] Description
[p206_c0008] The Fulton Street Station is a below-grade station with one straight center / island platform. The platform structure is cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platform is approximately 13'-6” throughout. At the south end of the southbound platform, the existing clearance is 46”. The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36”. The remaining 31” or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p206_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
> [p206_c0010] A photograph of a subway platform at Fulton Street Station. A yellow rectangular platform screen door (PSD) is positioned on the platform. A red line with arrows indicates a width of 31 inches from the platform edge to the wall. A white label 'PSD' is on the screen door, and a white label '15"' is on the platform floor. A red 'No Entry' sign is visible in the background.
[p206_c0011] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Fulton Street Station_

## Page 207

### [p207_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p207_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p207_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p207_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Wall Street Station)
### [p207_c0005] 1.21 – MR 333 | Wall Street Station
[p207_c0006] Summary: Wall Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 17" (see figure 1).
### [p207_c0007] Description
[p207_c0008] The Wall Street Station is a below-grade station with one center / island platform. The platform structure is cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platform is approximately 14'-0", narrowing to 6'-6" at the end. At several of the stairs, the existing clearance is 32". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 17" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement nor regular passenger movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p207_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
> [p207_c0010] A photograph of a subway platform at Wall Street Station. A yellow rectangular box labeled 'PSD' is placed on the platform, representing a Platform Screen Door. Red dimension lines indicate a width of 15 inches for the PSD and 17 inches for the remaining platform width. A 'Wall Street' sign is visible in the background.
[p207_c0011] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Wall Street Station_
[p207_c0012] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p207_c0013] _Page 40 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 208

### [p208_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p208_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p208_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p208_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Clark Street Station)
### [p208_c0005] 1.22 – MR 334 | Clark Street Station
[p208_c0006] Summary: Clark Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 32" minimum pinch point requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met at all stairs as the remaining width would be 9" (see figure 1)..
### [p208_c0007] Description
[p208_c0008] Clark Street Station is a below-grade station with one center / Island platform. The platform structure is cast in place concrete. The width of the platform is approximately 15'-6". At the two stairs, columns flanking the stairs leave 24" of clearance. The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 32". The remaining 9" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement nor regular passenger movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p208_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p208_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Clark Street Station_
[p208_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p208_c0012] _Page 41 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 209

### [p209_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p209_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p209_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p209_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Borough Hall Station)
### [p209_c0005] 1.23 – MR 335 | Borough Hall Station
[p209_c0006] Summary: Borough Hall Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 23" (see figure 1).
### [p209_c0007] Description
[p209_c0008] Borough Hall Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast in place concrete. The width of the platforms varies from 3'-2" to 11'- 8". At the south end of the southbound platform the corridor width to the platform wall is only 3'-2". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 23" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement nor regular passenger movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p209_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p209_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Borough Hall Station_
[p209_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p209_c0012] _Page 42 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 210

### [p210_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p210_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p210_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p210_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Hoyt Street Station)
### [p210_c0005] 1.24 – MR 336 | Hoyt Street Station
[p210_c0006] Summary: Hoyt Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 32" minimum pinch point requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met at the north end of the northbound as the remaining width would be 19" (see figure 1).
### [p210_c0007] Description
[p210_c0008] Hoyt Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast in place concrete. The width of the platforms varies from 11'-0" to 13'- 0", and narrowing at the ends. At the north end of the northbound platform, the existing clearance at the columns is 34". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 19" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement nor regular passenger movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p210_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
> [p210_c0010] A photograph of a subway platform. A yellow translucent rectangular unit, labeled 'PSD', is positioned on the platform. Red dimension lines are overlaid on the image. One line indicates a width of 15" for the PSD unit. Another line indicates a width of 19" for the remaining clearance between the PSD unit and a red support column. A person is visible inside the train car on the left side of the image.
[p210_c0011] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Hoyt Street Station_
[p210_c0012] _MTA New York City Transit_

## Page 211

### [p211_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p211_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p211_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p211_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Nevins Street Station)
### [p211_c0005] 1.25 – MR 337 | Nevins Street Station
[p211_c0006] Summary: Nevins Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as the columns which are located 20" from the platform edge would impede both access for maintenance and the ability to egress the train through the hinged emergency exit doors.
### [p211_c0007] Description
[p211_c0008] Nevins Street Station is a below-grade station with two center / island platforms ( see Figure 1 ). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. It is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to the presence of structural columns on the platforms which are within the envelope that the proposed PSD system(s) would occupy. The columns pictured in Figure 1 measure approximately 20" from the platform edge. While this dimension allows for the 15"-wide APG/PSD system, installation and maintenance cannot be carried out due to the lack of clear space. Furthermore, egress doors installed on an APG/PSD system would be obstructed by the present columns. Altering the proposed APG/PSD system to adapt to the existing conditions or relocating the present structural columns pose cost prohibitive scenarios
[p211_c0009] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Nevins Street Station_
[p211_c0010] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p211_c0011] _Page 44 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 212

### [p212_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p212_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p212_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr)
### [p212_c0004] 1.26 – MR 338 | Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr Station
[p212_c0005] Summary: Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr. Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 21" (see figure 1).
### [p212_c0006] Description
[p212_c0007] The Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr. Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms and a center / island platform. The 2 trains stop at the side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 6'-8" to 18'-0". The corridor width at the staircase at the south end of the southbound platform is 36". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 21" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p212_c0008] The non-compliant condition noted above could be remedied by moving the stopping location of the train. The proposal to move the stopping position of trains in specific stations would need to be studied by NYCT Signals Engineering to determine the impact to signals and signals equipment between that station and a series of adjacent stations all the way to the nearest interlocking. In many cases signals equipment at several locations would need to be relocated and rewired. The only way to fully determine this is to make an analysis of the existing signals system in that area.
[p212_c0009] Since that type of task is outside the scope of the PSD Feasibility Study, it is concluded that the current train stopping position is fixed. Given this condition, the ADA and/or Code analysis is being used for the feasibility analysis.
[p212_c0010] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p212_c0011] _Page 45 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 213

### [p213_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p213_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr)
> [p213_c0005] A photograph of a subway platform at Atlantic Avenue Barclay Center. A yellow rectangular box labeled 'PSD' is overlaid on the platform, indicating the proposed placement of a platform screen door. Red dimension lines with arrows show a width of 15 inches from the train to the box, and a depth of 21 inches from the box to a green support pillar. A person is standing on the platform in the background.
[p213_c0006] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Atlantic Ave Station_
[p213_c0007] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p213_c0008] _Page 46 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 214

### [p214_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p214_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p214_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations
### [p214_c0004] (Bergen Street Station)
### [p214_c0005] 1.27 – MR 339 | Bergen Street Station
[p214_c0006] Summary: Bergen Street Station is feasible for both APGs and PSDs. Platform edge reconstruction may be required to support the requirements of an APG system (see Appendix B). Existing power is adequate.
### [p214_c0007] Description
[p214_c0008] Bergen Street Station is a below ground station with two side platforms ( see Figure 1 ). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Columns are located only at the center of the platforms along the platform edge. Column faces measure approximately 3'-8" from the platform edge. The platform widths are approximately 7-10" throughout. Ceiling heights measure no less than 7'-6" throughout.
[p214_c0009] Full Height PSDs: Full height PSDs will require lateral structural bracing to the station ceiling as well as reconfiguration of existing ceiling-mounted systems to address edge-of-platform requirements of lighting, entrapment prevention sensors, CCTV, and standard NYCT wayfinding signage.
[p214_c0010] Half Height PSDs (aka APGs): This system is less likely to affect existing lighting and other systems on the ceiling. Depending on the half height PSD design, sensors may be ceiling-mounted or mounted on the APG unit itself. In any case, there will be a CCTV camera over each motorized sliding door which will need to be in coordination with existing or replacement lighting.
### [p214_c0011] Equipment Room
[p214_c0012] The equipment room can be located at the southbound control area of the station ( see Figure 1, Figure 2 ). The proposed room dimensions are 27'-6" x 7'-0".
### [p214_c0013] Track Layout
[p214_c0014] Tracks are tangent. Therefore we are not expecting that gaps between the platform and train will exacerbate the gap between the train doors and the PSDs. However, per NYCT standards, the Limiting Line of Line Equipment (LLE) would necessitate the placement of PSDs sufficiently distant from the train doors to create gaps that would have to be addressed by entrapment prevention measures.
### [p214_c0015] Platform Edge Condition
[p214_c0016] The platform edges were reconstructed within the past thirty years. From our limited visual inspection and our knowledge of repair strategies used in station rehabilitations over the last thirty years, structural work would at a minimum be required for the installation of an APG system.
[p214_c0017] _Page 47 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 217

### [p217_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p217_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p217_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Bergen Street Station)
### [p217_c0004] Platform obstructions within 5' of edge:
- [p217_c0005] • Columns
[p217_c0006] Please note that in the ADA boarding zones, existing columns obstruct the 60" circle requirement discussed in the ADA summary in Appendix A. The installation of a PSD system would not further exacerbate these conditions.
### [p217_c0007] Lighting:
[p217_c0008] Existing lighting: Throughout both platforms there are linear fluorescent fixtures mounted parallel to the platform edge. Depending on the specific APG/PSD design used, there could be no or minimal alterations to the existing lighting configuration
### [p217_c0009] Power:
[p217_c0010] The Reserve EDR was not accessible at the time of survey. However, the Normal electrical service has adequate electrical capacity to support the implementation of an APG/PSD system. We do not consider a lack of adequate existing power to be a determining factor of future feasibility. If in the future, a PSD/APG project is to be implemented at this station, and it is determined at that time that an upgrade in power service to the station is required, it would simply mean an increased cost to the project. Below in Table 1 please see the Power Capacity Analysis for this station.
[p217_c0011] _Station Power Capacity Analysis (normal service)_
[p217_c0012]

```text
Station Name Bergen Street Peak Demand Load from ConEd Report, Last 20 Months, (kW) 40.8 Apparent Power (kVA) 51.0 Station Peak Demand Load, Max Current, (A) 141.7 Maximum Amount of Doors 40.0 PSD Total Load Including All Miscellaneous Loads, (A) 121.6 Total Load (Station Peak + PSD), (A) 263 Station Service Power Capacity, (Main SB or SG Rating), (A) 800 Service Spare Capacity, (A) 537 Is Electrical Service Adequate? Yes Notes Service capacity data is based on observations of breaker schedule.
```
[p217_c0013] _Table 1. Normal Service Power Capacity Analysis_
[p217_c0014] _Page 50 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 218

### [p218_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p218_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p218_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Bergen Street Station)
### [p218_c0004] Historic Restrictions:
[p218_c0005] None
### [p218_c0006] Rough Order-of-Magnitude Cost Estimate:
[p218_c0007] The rough order-of-magnitude (ROM) cost estimate is based on a summary of anticipated costs developed through a review of field conditions, analysis of space constraints and existing documentation. It is not based upon an actual conceptual design. The basis of estimate assumptions are listed in the attached ROM estimate. The total cost for this station is estimated to be $28.9M to install APGs and $33.3M to install PSDs (See Appendix E)
[p218_c0008] _New York City Transit_
[p218_c0009] _Page 51 of 88 March 11, 2019_

### [p219_c0005] 1.28 – MR 340 | Grand Army Street Station
[p219_c0006] Summary: Grand Army Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 29" (see figure 1).
### [p219_c0007] Description
[p219_c0008] The Grand Army Street Station is a below-grade station with one straight center/island platform. The platform structure is cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platform is approximately 32'-4" throughout. The corridor width at this station's western end is 44". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 29" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p219_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.

## Page 220

### [p220_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p220_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p220_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p220_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Eastern Parkway Brooklyn Street Station)
### [p220_c0005] 1.29 – MR 341 | Eastern Parkway Brooklyn Street Station
[p220_c0006] Summary: Eastern Parkway Brooklyn Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the northbound platform as the existing width is 5'-6" (see figure 1).
### [p220_c0007] Description
[p220_c0008] Eastern Parkway Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 5'-6" to 11'-10".
[p220_c0009] Platform width at the ends of the platforms is 5'-6" or 66". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
[p220_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition Eastern Parkway Station_
[p220_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p220_c0012] _Page 53 of 88 March 11, 2019_

### [p221_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Franklin Avenue Botanic Garden Station)
### [p221_c0005] 1.30 – MR 342 | Franklin Avenue Botanic Garden Station
[p221_c0006] Summary: Franklin Avenue Botanic Garden Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as the columns which are located 16" from the platform edge would impede both access for maintenance and the ability to egress the train through the hinged emergency exit doors.
### [p221_c0007] Description:
[p221_c0008] Franklin Avenue Station is a below-grade station consisting of two center / island platforms. It is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to the presence of structural columns on the platforms which are within the envelope that the proposed PSD system(s) would occupy. The column pictured in Figure 1 measures approximately 16" from the platform edge. While this dimension allows for the 15"-wide APG/PSD system, installation and maintenance cannot be carried out due to the lack of clear space. Furthermore, egress doors installed on an APG/PSD system would be obstructed by the present columns. Altering the proposed APG/PSD system to adapt to the existing conditions or relocating the present structural columns pose cost prohibitive scenarios.
[p221_c0009] _Figure 1 – Column 16" from the edge Franklin Avenue Station_
[p221_c0010] _MTA New York City Transit_

## Page 222

### [p222_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p222_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p222_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p222_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (President Street Station)
### [p222_c0005] 1.31 – MR 353 | President Street Station
[p222_c0006] Summary: President Street is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36” minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met at five platform stairs as the remaining width would be 27” (see figure 1).
### [p222_c0007] Description
[p222_c0008] President Street Station is a below-grade station with one straight center / island platform. The platform structure is cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platform is approximately 20'-0” throughout. The corridor width at southern end of this platform is 42”. The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36”. The remaining 27” or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement nor regular passenger movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p222_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p222_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition President Street Station_
[p222_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p222_c0012] _Page 55 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 223

### [p223_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p223_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p223_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Sterling Street Station)
### [p223_c0004] 1.32 – MR 354 | Sterling Street Station
[p223_c0005] Summary: Sterling Street Station is feasible for both APGs and PSDs. Platform edge reconstruction may be required to support the requirements of an APG system (see Appendix B). Existing power capacity could not be ascertained due to inaccessibility during survey. However, a lack of adequate existing power is not considered to be a determining factor of future feasibility.
### [p223_c0006] Description
[p223_c0007] Sterling Street Station is a below-grade station with two side platforms ( see Figure 1 ). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Columns are located throughout the length of the platforms along the platform edge. The platform widths are approximately 11-10" throughout. Ceiling heights measure no less than 7'-6" throughout.
[p223_c0008] Full Height PSDs: Full height PSDs will require lateral structural bracing to the station ceiling as well as reconfiguration of existing ceiling-mounted systems to address edge-of-platform requirements of lighting, entrapment prevention sensors, CCTV, and standard NYCT wayfinding signage.
[p223_c0009] Half Height PSDs (aka APGs): This system is less likely to affect existing lighting and other systems on the ceiling. Depending on the half height PSD design, sensors may be ceiling-mounted or mounted on the APG unit itself. In any case, there will be a CCTV camera over each motorized sliding door which will need to be in coordination with existing or replacement lighting.
### [p223_c0010] Equipment Room
[p223_c0011] The equipment room can be located in the station mezzanine ( see Figure 1, Figure 2 ). The proposed room dimensions are 27'-6" x 7'-0".
### [p223_c0012] Track Layout
[p223_c0013] Tracks are tangent. Therefore we are not expecting that gaps between the platform and train will exacerbate the gap between the train doors and the PSDs. However, per NYCT standards, the Limiting Line of Line Equipment (LLLE) would necessitate the placement of PSDs sufficiently distant from the train doors to create gaps that would have to be addressed by entrapment prevention measures.
### [p223_c0014] Platform Edge Condition
[p223_c0015] The platform edges appear to be original to the station construction. From our limited visual inspection and our knowledge of repair strategies used in station rehabilitations over the last thirty years, structural work would be required for the installation of both an APG and PSD system.
[p223_c0016] _Page 56 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 226

### [p226_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p226_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p226_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Sterling Street Station)
### [p226_c0004] Platform obstructions within 5' of edge:
- [p226_c0005] • Columns
[p226_c0006] Please note that in the ADA boarding zones, existing columns obstruct the 60" circle requirement discussed in the ADA summary in Appendix A. The installation of a PSD system would not further exacerbate these conditions.
### [p226_c0007] Lighting:
[p226_c0008] Existing lighting: Throughout both platforms there are linear florescent fixtures mounted parallel to the platform edge. Depending on the specific APG/PSD design used, there could be no or minimal alterations to the existing lighting configuration
### [p226_c0009] Power:
[p226_c0010] An analysis of adequate electrical power at this station could not be performed due to inaccessibility during survey. Please note, a lack of adequate existing power is not considered to be a determining factor of future feasibility. If in the future, a PSD/APG project is to be implemented at this station, and it is determined at that time that an upgrade in power service to the station is required, it would simply mean an increased cost to the project.
### [p226_c0011] Historic Restrictions:
[p226_c0012] None
### [p226_c0013] Rough Order-of-Magnitude Cost Estimate:
[p226_c0014] The rough order-of-magnitude (ROM) cost estimate is based on a summary of anticipated costs developed through a review of field conditions, analysis of space constraints and existing documentation. It is not based upon an actual conceptual design. The basis of estimate assumptions are listed in the attached ROM estimate. The total cost for this station is estimated to be $26.7M to install APGs and $33.4M to install PSDs (See Appendix E)
[p226_c0015] _Page 59 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 227

### [p227_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p227_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p227_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p227_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Winthrop Street Station)
### [p227_c0005] 1.33 – MR 355 | Winthrop Street Station
[p227_c0006] Summary: Winthrop Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 29" (see figure 1).
### [p227_c0007] Description
[p227_c0008] The Winthrop Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 3'-8" to 11'-10". The corridor width at the southbound end of the platforms is 3'-8". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 29" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p227_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p227_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Winthrop Street Station_
[p227_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p227_c0012] _Page 60 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 228

[p228_c0001] Table of Contents NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516 Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for ‘2’ Line Stations (Church Avenue Station) 1.34 – MR 356 | Church Avenue Station Summary: Church Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to lack of available space for the PSD equipment room. Description Church Avenue Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. There is a single row of columns along each platform edge. The platform width varies from 7’-10” to 11’-10” throughout. Due to the extremely limited width of the existing platforms and control areas, there is no available space for the equipment room. Figure 2 below shows the minimum width required for construction of a PSD equipment room if placed on the platform. Figure 1, below, demonstrates the lack of available space within the southbound control area. The northbound control area is similar. Figure 1 – Congested / Narrow Station Plan Church Avenue Station Figure 2 – Diagram demonstrating minimum platform width dimensions Church Avenue Station Page 61 of 88 March 11, 2019

## Page 229

### [p229_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p229_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p229_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p229_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Beverly Road Station)
### [p229_c0005] 1.35 – MR 357 | Beverly Road Station
[p229_c0006] Summary: Beverly Road Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the north end of the northbound platform as the existing width is 5'-9" (see figure 1).
### [p229_c0007] Description
[p229_c0008] Beverly Road Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 5'-9" to 11'-10".
[p229_c0009] Platform width at the north end of the northbound platform is 5'-9" or 69". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
> [p229_c0010] A photograph of a subway platform with a yellow translucent PSD (Platform Screen Door) installed. Red dimension lines indicate the width of the platform is 5'-9" (69 inches) and the width of the PSD is 15 inches. The remaining width of the corridor is 54 inches. The PSD is labeled 'PSD' on its side.
[p229_c0011] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition Beverly Road Station_
[p229_c0012] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p229_c0013] _Page 62 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 230

### [p230_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p230_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p230_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p230_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Newkirk Avenue Station)
### [p230_c0005] 1.36 – MR 358 | Newkirk Avenue Station
[p230_c0006] Summary: Newkirk Avenue Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the both platforms as the existing width is 5'-9" (see figure 1).
### [p230_c0007] Description
[p230_c0008] Newkirk Avenue Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 5'-9" to 11'-10".
[p230_c0009] Platform width at the ends of the northbound & southbound platform are 5'-9" or 69". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
[p230_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition Newkirk Avenue Station_
[p230_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p230_c0012] _Page 63 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 231

### [p231_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p231_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p231_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p231_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Flatbush Avenue Brooklyn College Station)
### [p231_c0005] 1.37 – MR 359 | Flatbush Avenue Brooklyn College Station
[p231_c0006] Summary: Flatbush Avenue Brooklyn College Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 13" (see figure 1).
### [p231_c0007] Description
[p231_c0008] The Flatbush Avenue Brooklyn College Station is a below-grade terminus station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The corridor width at the northern end of the northbound platform is 2'-4". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 13" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p231_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p231_c0010] _Figure 1 – Area of ADA non-compliance Flatbush Avenue Brooklyn College Station_
[p231_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p231_c0012] _Page 64 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 232

### [p232_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p232_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p232_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p232_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (241 st Street Wakefield Station)
### [p232_c0005] 1.38 – MR 416 | 241 st Street Wakefield Station
[p232_c0006] Summary: 241 st Street Wakefield is not feasible for APGs or PSDs due to the elevated Precast T-Beam platform which has been deemed structurally insufficient to carry the load of a platform edge barrier system (see Appendix B and figure 1).
### [p232_c0007] Description
[p232_c0008] The 241 st Street Station is an elevated terminus station with two closed side platforms and one open center/island platform. The platform structures are precast concrete. The width of the center platform is approximately 15'-0" throughout. The platform is straight with one row of columns supporting its respective station canopies. This station is also infeasible due to non-compliant ADA dimensions at south end of the platforms, where the existing 30" of width will be reduced to 15" with the installation of PSDs. See figure 1 & 2 for reference.
> [p232_c0009] A photograph showing a side view of a silver and blue subway train car on an elevated platform. The platform has a yellow tactile paving strip. The train car's door is open, and the interior is visible. The platform edge is close to the train, illustrating a non-compliant ADA condition.
[p232_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA Condition 241 st Street Station_
> [p232_c0011] A photograph showing a close-up view of the elevated platform structure. The platform is made of precast concrete slabs supported by a series of columns. The structure is elevated above the tracks, and the platform edge is visible.
[p232_c0012] _Figure 2 – Precast Slab 241 st Street Station_
[p232_c0013] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p232_c0014] _Page 65 of 88 March 11, 2019_

[p233_c0008] The 238th Street Station is an elevated station with two side platforms. The platform structures are precast concrete. The width of the platforms varies from approximately 12'-0" to 12'-4". The platforms are straight with one row of columns supporting their respective station canopies. See figure 1 & 2 for reference.

### [p245_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (Freeman Street Station)
### [p245_c0005] 1.51 – MR 429 | Freeman Street Station
[p245_c0006] Summary: Freeman Street is not feasible for APGs or PSDs due to the elevated Precast T-Beam platform which has been deemed structurally insufficient to carry the load of a platform edge barrier system (see Appendix B and figure 1).
### [p245_c0007] Description
[p245_c0008] The Freeman Street Station is an elevated station with two side platforms. The platform structures are precast concrete. The width of the platforms varies from approximately 7'-0" to 13'-8". The platforms are mildly curved with one row of columns supporting their respective station canopies See figure 1 & 2 for reference.
> [p245_c0009] A photograph showing the general condition of the Freeman Street Station platform. It features a concrete platform with a yellow tactile strip along the edge, a blue metal railing, and several streetlights. In the background, there are brick buildings and a sign that says "Children's".
[p245_c0010] _Figure 1 – General Station Condition Freeman Street Station_
> [p245_c0011] A photograph showing a close-up view of the precast slab structure of the Freeman Street Station platform. It shows the concrete beams and columns supporting the platform, with some scaffolding visible.
[p245_c0012] _Figure 2 – Precast Slab Freeman Street Station_
[p245_c0013] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p245_c0014] _Page 78 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 250

### [p250_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p250_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p250_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p250_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (3rd Avenue 149th Street)
### [p250_c0005] 1.56 – MR 434 | 3rd Avenue 149th Street Station
[p250_c0006] Summary: 3rd Avenue 149th Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as the columns which are located 18" from the platform edge would impede both access for maintenance and the ability to egress the train through the hinged emergency exit doors.
### [p250_c0007] Description:
[p250_c0008] 3rd Avenue 149th Street Station is a below-grade station consisting of two side platforms. It is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to the presence of structural columns on the platforms which are within the envelope that the proposed PSD system(s) would occupy. The columns pictured in Figure 1 measure approximately 18" from the platform edge. While this dimension allows for the 15"-wide APG/PSD system, installation and maintenance cannot be carried out due to the lack of clear space. Furthermore, egress doors installed on an APG/PSD system would be obstructed by the present columns. Altering the proposed APG/PSD system to adapt to the existing conditions or relocating the present structural columns pose cost prohibitive scenarios.
[p250_c0009] _Figure 1 – Column 18" from the edge 3rd Avenue 149th Street Station_
[p250_c0010] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p250_c0011] _Page 83 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 251

### [p251_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p251_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p251_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p251_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (149 th Street Grand Concourse Station)
### [p251_c0005] 1.57 – MR 435 | 149 th Street Grand Concourse Station
[p251_c0006] Summary: 149 th Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA complaint wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 35" (see figure 1).
### [p251_c0007] Description
[p251_c0008] The 149 th Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The corridor width at the western ends of the platforms is 4'-2". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 35" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p251_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p251_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant ADA condition 149 th Street Station_
[p251_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p251_c0012] _Page 84 of 88 March 11, 2019_

## Page 255

### [p255_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p255_c0002] STV 100 years
### [p255_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p255_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '2' Line Stations (110 th Street Central Park North Station)
### [p255_c0005] 1.61 – MR 441 | 110 th Street Central Park North Station
[p255_c0006] Summary: 110 th Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the northbound platform as the existing width is 5'-0" (see figure 1).
### [p255_c0007] Description
[p255_c0008] 110 th Street Station is a below-grade station with one center/island platform. The platform structure is cast-in-place concrete. The width of the corridors at this station ranges from 5'-0" to 21'-6".
[p255_c0009] The corridor width at the center of this platform adjacent to the control area is constrained to a 5'-0" or 60". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
[p255_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition 110 th Street Station_
[p255_c0011] _MTA New York City Transit_
[p255_c0012] _Page 88 of 88 March 11, 2019_

### [p257_c0007] Tier 2-3 Technology Assessment (Summary of Sections 2.0 through 5.0 and Berthing Report)
[p257_c0008] Issued: 9/15/17
