### [p461_c0002] The logo for the Southern Transportation Vehicle (STV) 100th anniversary. It features a black square with a white stylized 'S' inside, followed by the letters 'STV' in a bold, black, sans-serif font. To the right of 'STV' is a red, cursive script '100 years'.
### [p461_c0003] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors—Contract C-32516
### [p461_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations
### [p461_c0005] 1.0 Station Assessments
[p461_c0006] _The MTA logo, consisting of a blue circle with the white letters 'MTA' inside. New York City Transit_
[p461_c0007] _Page 7 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 462

### [p462_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p462_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p462_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Nevins Street Station)
### [p462_c0004] 1.01 – MR 337 | Nevins Street Station
[p462_c0005] Summary: Nevins Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as the columns which are located 20" from the platform edge would impede both access for maintenance and the ability to egress the train through the hinged emergency exit doors.
### [p462_c0006] Description
[p462_c0007] Nevins Street Station is a below-grade station with two center / island platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. It is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to the presence of structural columns on the platforms which are within the envelope that the proposed PSD system(s) would occupy. The columns pictured in Figure 1 measure approximately 20" from the platform edge. While this dimension allows for the 15"-wide APG/PSD system, installation and maintenance cannot be carried out due to the lack of clear space. Furthermore, egress doors installed on an APG/PSD system would be obstructed by the present columns. Altering the proposed APG/PSD system to adapt to the existing conditions or relocating the present structural columns pose cost prohibitive scenarios.
> [p462_c0008] A photograph of a subway platform at Nevins Street Station. The platform is paved with light-colored square tiles and has a yellow tactile paving strip along the edge. Several large, green-painted structural columns are visible, supporting the platform ceiling. A subway train is partially visible on the right side of the platform. The columns are positioned very close to the platform edge, approximately 20 inches away, which would obstruct the installation of platform screen doors.
[p462_c0009] _Figure 1 – Columns at 20" from platform edge Nevins Street Station_
[p462_c0010] _Page 8 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 463

### [p463_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p463_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p463_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr)
### [p463_c0004] 1.02 – MR 338 | Atlantic Avenue Barclay Center Station
[p463_c0005] Summary: Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr. Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as the columns which are located 16" from the platform edge would impede both access for maintenance and the ability to egress the train through the hinged emergency exit doors.
### [p463_c0006] Description:
[p463_c0007] Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr. is a below-grade station consisting of one center / island platform (the No. 2&3 trains utilize separate side platforms). This report concerns only the No. 4&5 train platform. It is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to the presence of structural columns on the platforms which are within the envelope that the proposed PSD system(s) would occupy. The column pictured in Figure 1 measures approximately 16" from the platform edge. While this dimension allows for the 15"-wide APG/PSD system, installation and maintenance cannot be carried out due to the lack of clear space. Furthermore, egress doors installed on an APG/PSD system would be obstructed by the present columns. Altering the proposed APG/PSD system to adapt to the existing conditions or relocating the present structural columns pose cost prohibitive scenarios.
[p463_c0008] _Figure 1 – Column 16" from the edge Atlantic Avenue Barclay Ctr. Station_
[p463_c0009] _Page 9 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 464

### [p464_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p464_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p464_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Bergen Street Station)
### [p464_c0004] 1.03 – MR 339 | Bergen Street Station
[p464_c0005] Summary: Bergen Street Station is feasible for both APGs and PSDs. Platform edge reconstruction may be required to support the requirements of an APG system (see Appendix B). Existing power is adequate.
### [p464_c0006] Description
[p464_c0007] Bergen Street Station is a below-grade station with two side platforms ( see Figure 1 ). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Columns are located only at the center of the platforms along the platform edge. Column faces measure approximately 3'-8" from the platform edge. The platform widths are approximately 7-10" throughout. Ceiling heights measure no less than 7'-6" throughout.
[p464_c0008] Full Height PSDs: Full height PSDs will require lateral structural bracing to the station ceiling as well as reconfiguration of existing ceiling-mounted systems to address edge-of-platform requirements of lighting, entrapment prevention sensors, CCTV, and standard NYCT wayfinding signage.
[p464_c0009] Half Height PSDs (aka APGs): This system is less likely to affect existing lighting and other systems on the ceiling. Depending on the half height PSD design, sensors may be ceiling-mounted or mounted on the APG unit itself. In any case, there will be a CCTV camera over each motorized sliding door which will need to be in coordination with existing or replacement lighting.
### [p464_c0010] Equipment Room
[p464_c0011] The equipment room can be located at the southbound control area of the station ( see Figure 1, Figure 2 ). The proposed room dimensions are 27'-6" x 7'-0".
### [p464_c0012] Track Layout
[p464_c0013] Tracks are tangent. Therefore we are not expecting that gaps between the platform and train will exacerbate the gap between the train doors and the PSDs. However, per NYCT standards, the Limiting Line of Line Equipment (LLE) would necessitate the placement of PSDs sufficiently distant from the train doors to create gaps that would have to be addressed by entrapment prevention measures.
### [p464_c0014] Platform Edge Condition
[p464_c0015] The platform edges were reconstructed within the past thirty years. From our limited visual inspection and our knowledge of repair strategies used in station rehabilitations over the last thirty years, structural work would at a minimum be required for the installation of an APG system.
[p464_c0016] _Page 10 of 81 March 29, 2019_

The drawing is labeled 'Figure 1 – Overall Station Plan Bergen Street Station' at the bottom.

## Page 467

### [p467_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p467_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p467_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Bergen Street Station)
### [p467_c0004] Platform obstructions within 5' of edge:
- [p467_c0005] • Columns
[p467_c0006] Please note that in the ADA boarding zones, existing columns obstruct the 60" circle requirement discussed in the ADA summary in Appendix A. The installation of a PSD system would not further exacerbate these conditions.
### [p467_c0007] Lighting:
[p467_c0008] Existing lighting: Throughout both platforms there are linear florescent fixtures mounted parallel to the platform edge. Depending on the specific APG/PSD design used, there could be no or minimal alterations to the existing lighting configuration
### [p467_c0009] Power:
[p467_c0010] This station has adequate electrical capacity to support the implementation of an APG/PSD system. An analysis of electrical reserve service could not be performed due to inaccessibility during survey. We do not consider a lack of adequate existing power to be a determining factor of future feasibility. If in the future, a PSD/APG project is to be implemented at this station, and it is determined at that time that an upgrade in power service to the station is required, it would simply mean an increased cost to the project. Below in Table 1 please see the Power Capacity Analysis for this station.
[p467_c0011]

```text
Station Power Capacity Analysis (Normal Service) Station Name Bergen Street Peak Demand Load from ConEd Report, (kW) 40.8 Apparent Power (kVA) 51.0 Station Peak Demand Load, Max Current, (A) 141.7 Maximum Amount of Doors 40.0 PSD Total Load Including All Miscellaneous Loads, (A) 121.6 Total Load (Station Peak + PSD), (A) 263 Station Service Power Capacity, (Main SB or SG Rating), (A) 800 Service Spare Capacity, (A) 537 Is Electrical Service Adequate? Yes Notes Service rating is based on the 1 line diagram, having 800A fuses at Service switch. The analysis is based on available Normal meter reading. (No access to Reserve room and no Reserve meter reading provided)
```
[p467_c0012] _Table 1. Normal Service Power Capacity Analysis_
[p467_c0013] _Page 13 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 468

### [p468_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p468_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p468_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Bergen Street Station)
### [p468_c0004] Historic Restrictions:
[p468_c0005] None
### [p468_c0006] Rough Order-of-Magnitude Cost Estimate:
[p468_c0007] The rough order-of-magnitude (ROM) cost estimate is based on a summary of anticipated costs developed through a review of field conditions, analysis of space constraints and existing documentation. It is not based upon an actual conceptual design. The basis of estimate assumptions are listed in the attached ROM estimate. The total cost for this station is estimated to be $26.9M to install APGs and $33.3M to install PSDs (See Appendix E)
[p468_c0008] _New York City Transit_
[p468_c0009] _Page 14 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 469

### [p469_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p469_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p469_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Grand Army Plaza Station)
### [p469_c0004] 1.04 – MR 340 | Grand Army Plaza Station
[p469_c0005] Summary: Grand Army Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA compliant wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 29" (see figure 1).
### [p469_c0006] Description
[p469_c0007] The Grand Army Street Station is a below-grade station with one straight center / island platform. The platform structure is cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platform is approximately 32'-4" throughout. The corridor width at this station's western end is 44". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce this width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 29" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p469_c0008] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p469_c0009] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition Grand Army Plaza Station_
[p469_c0010] _Page 15 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 470

### [p470_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p470_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p470_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Eastern Parkway Brooklyn Street Station)
### [p470_c0004] 1.05 – MR 341 | Eastern Parkway Station
[p470_c0005] Summary: Eastern Parkway Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the northbound platform as the existing width is 5'-6" (see figure 1).
### [p470_c0006] Description
[p470_c0007] Eastern Parkway Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 5'-6" to 11'-10".
[p470_c0008] Platform width at the ends of the platforms is 5'-6" or 66". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
[p470_c0009] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition Eastern Parkway Station_
[p470_c0010] _Page 16 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 472

### [p472_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p472_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p472_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for ‘4’ Line Stations (Nostrand Avenue Eastern Pkwy)
### [p472_c0004] 1.07 – MR 343 | Nostrand Avenue / Eastern Pkwy
[p472_c0005] Summary: Nostrand Avenue Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to lack of available space for the PSD equipment room.
### [p472_c0006] Description
[p472_c0007] Nostrand Avenue Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Platform widths vary from 7’-10” to 11’-10”. There is a single row of columns on each platform.
[p472_c0008] Due to the limited width of the existing platforms, there is no available space for the equipment room. Figure 2 below shows the minimum width required (12’-11”) for construction of a PSD equipment room on a station platform. Figure 1, below, demonstrates the lack of available space within the main control area.
[p472_c0009] _Figure 1 – Congested/Narrow Station Plan Nostrand Avenue Eastern Pkwy_
[p472_c0010] _Figure 2 – Diagram demonstrating minimum platform width dimensions (A Division train shown; B Division requires same dimension)_
[p472_c0011] _Page 18 of 81 March 29, 2019_

### [p474_c0004] 1.09 – MR 345 | Crown Heights / Utica Avenue
[p474_c0005] Summary: Crown Heights Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as the columns which are located 12" from the platform edge would impede both access for maintenance and the ability to egress the train through the hinged emergency exit doors.
### [p474_c0006] Description:
[p474_c0007] Crown Heights Station is a below-grade station consisting of two center / island platforms. It is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to the presence of structural columns on the platform which are within the envelope that the proposed PSD system(s) would occupy. The columns pictured in Figure 1 measure approximately 12" from the platform edge. This dimension does not allow for installation and maintenance of the 15"-wide APG/PSD system. Furthermore, egress doors installed on an APG/PSD system would be obstructed by the present columns. Altering the proposed APG/PSD system to adapt to the existing conditions or relocating the present structural columns pose cost prohibitive scenarios.

### [p475_c0004] 1.10 – MR 346 | Sutter Avenue
[p475_c0005] Summary: Sutter Avenue Station is not feasible for APGs or PSDs due to the elevated Precast T-Beam platform which has been deemed structurally insufficient to carry the load of a platform edge barrier system (see Appendix B).
### [p475_c0006] Description
[p475_c0007] The Sutter Avenue Station is an elevated station with two side platforms. The platform structures are precast concrete. The width of the platforms varies from approximately 8'-10" to 9'-10". The platforms are straight with one row of columns inset in the windscreen. See figure 1 & 2 for reference.
[p475_c0008] _Figure 1 – General Station Condition Sutter Avenue Station_
[p475_c0009] _Figure 2 – Precast Slab Sutter Avenue Station_
[p475_c0010] _Page 21 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 483

### [p483_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p483_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p483_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (Mosholu Parkway Station)
### [p483_c0004] 1.18 – MR 379 | Mosholu Parkway
[p483_c0005] Summary: Mosholu Parkway Station is not feasible for APGs or PSDs due to the elevated Precast T-Beam platform which has been deemed structurally insufficient to carry the load of a platform edge barrier system (see Appendix B).
### [p483_c0006] Description
[p483_c0007] The Mosholu Parkway Station is an elevated station with two side platforms. The platform structures are precast concrete. The width of the platforms is approximately 14'-10" throughout. The platforms are straight with one row of columns inset in the windscreen and another set adjacent to the platform edge. See figure 1 & 2 for reference.

[p483_c0009] _Figure 2 – Precast Slab Mosholu Parkway Station_

## Page 494

### [p494_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p494_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p494_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (149th Street Grand Concourse Station)
### [p494_c0004] 1.29 – MR 390 | 149 th Street Grand Concourse
[p494_c0005] Summary: The 149 th Street Grand Concourse Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the south end of the northbound platform as the existing width is 4'-6" (see figure 1).
### [p494_c0006] Description
[p494_c0007] The 149th Street Grand Concourse Upper Level Station is a below-grade station with two straight center/island platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 4'-6" to 19'-6".
[p494_c0008] The platform width at the southbound end of the northbound platform is 4'-6" or 54". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
[p494_c0009] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition 149 th Street Grand Concourse Station_
[p494_c0010] _Page 40 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 495

### [p495_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p495_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p495_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (138 th Street Grand Concourse Station)
### [p495_c0004] 1.30 – MR 391 | 138 th Street Grand Concourse
[p495_c0005] Summary: 138 th Street Grand Concourse Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA compliant wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 25" (see figure 1).
### [p495_c0006] Description
[p495_c0007] The 138 th Street Grand Concourse Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 3'-4" to 11'-6". The implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce the lesser width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 25" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p495_c0008] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p495_c0009] _Figure 1 – Non-compliant ADA condition 138 th Street Grand Concourse Station_
[p495_c0010] _Page 41 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 496

### [p496_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p496_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p496_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (125 th Street Station)
### [p496_c0004] 1.31 – MR 392 | 125 th Street
[p496_c0005] Summary: 125 th Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant ADA conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 36" minimum corridor requirement for ADA compliant wheelchair movement would not be met as the remaining width would be 25" (see figure 1).
### [p496_c0006] Description
[p496_c0007] 125 th Street Station is a below-grade station with two levels of straight center / island platforms. The no. 4 train utilizes both the upper and lower level. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 3'-4" to 29'-8". At the lower platform, the implementation of a platform edge barrier would reduce the lesser width below the required minimum of 36". The remaining 25" or less* would not allow for ADA compliant wheelchair movement. See figure 1 for reference.
[p496_c0008] The upper platform is infeasible for both APGs and PSDs due to the presence of structural columns on the platform which are within the envelope that the proposed PSD system(s) would occupy. The columns pictured in Figure 2 measure approximately 16" from the platform edge. While this dimension allows for the 15"-wide APG/PSD system, installation and maintenance cannot be carried out due to the lack of clear space. Furthermore, egress doors installed on an APG/PSD system would be obstructed by the present columns.
[p496_c0009] *Please note that the ADA clearance measurements are subject to a slight decrease due to the required placement of PSD/APG equipment outside the Limiting line of line equipment (LLLE), which is dictated by the dynamic envelope of the trains.
[p496_c0010] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant ADA clearance North end of lower platform; 125 th Street Station_
[p496_c0011] _Figure 2 – Columns at 16" from platform edge South end of upper platform_
[p496_c0012] _Page 42 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 497

### [p497_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p497_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p497_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (116 th Street Station)
### [p497_c0004] 1.32 – MR 393 | 116 th Street
[p497_c0005] Summary: 116 th Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to lack of available space for the PSD equipment room.
### [p497_c0006] Description
[p497_c0007] 116 th Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Platform widths vary from 7'-8" to 11'-10". There is a single row of columns on each platform.
[p497_c0008] Due to the limited width of the existing platforms, there is no available space for the equipment room. Figure 2 below shows the minimum width required (12'-11") for construction of a PSD equipment room on a station platform. Figure 1, below, demonstrates the lack of available space within the main control area.
[p497_c0009] _Figure 1 – Congested/Narrow Station Plan 116 th Street Station_
[p497_c0010] _Figure 2 – Diagram demonstrating minimum platform width dimensions (A Division train shown; B Division requires same dimension)_
[p497_c0011] _Page 43 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 498

### [p498_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for ‘4’ Line Stations (110 th Street Station)
### [p498_c0004] 1.33 – MR 394 | 110 th Street
[p498_c0005] Summary: 110 th Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs due to lack of available space for the PSD equipment room.
### [p498_c0006] Description
[p498_c0007] 110 th Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. Platform widths vary from 7’-8” to 8’-0”. There is a single row of columns on each platform.
[p498_c0008] Due to the limited width of the existing platforms, there is no available space for the equipment room. Figure 2 below shows the minimum width required (12’-11”) for construction of a PSD equipment room on a station platform. Figure 1, below, demonstrates the lack of available space within the main control area.
[p498_c0009] _Figure 1 – Congested/Narrow Station Plan 110 th Street Station_
[p498_c0010] _Figure 2 – Diagram demonstrating minimum platform width dimensions (A Division train shown; B Division requires same dimension)_

## Page 499

### [p499_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p499_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p499_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (103 rd Street Station)
### [p499_c0004] 1.34 – MR 395 | 103 rd Street
[p499_c0005] Summary: 103 rd Street Station is feasible for both APGs and PSDs. Platform edge reconstruction may be required to support the requirements of an APG system (see Appendix B). Existing power is adequate.
### [p499_c0006] Description
[p499_c0007] 103 rd Street Station is a below ground station with two side platforms (see Figure 1). The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. There are no columns located throughout the platforms. The platform widths are approximately 7-4" throughout. Ceiling heights measure no less than 7'-6" throughout.
[p499_c0008] Full Height PSDs: Full height PSDs will require lateral structural bracing to the station ceiling as well as reconfiguration of existing ceiling-mounted systems to address edge-of-platform requirements of lighting, entrapment prevention sensors, CCTV, and standard NYCT wayfinding signage.
[p499_c0009] Half Height PSDs (aka APGs): This system is less likely to affect existing lighting and other systems on the ceiling. Depending on the half height PSD design, sensors may be ceiling-mounted or mounted on the APG unit itself. In any case, there will be a CCTV camera over each motorized sliding door which will need to be in coordination with existing or replacement lighting.
### [p499_c0010] Equipment Room
[p499_c0011] The equipment room can be located at the mezzanine control area of the station (see Figure 1, Figure 2). The proposed room dimensions are 27'-6" x 7'-0".
### [p499_c0012] Track Layout
[p499_c0013] Tracks are tangent. Therefore we are not expecting that gaps between the platform and train will exacerbate the gap between the train doors and the PSDs. However, per NYCT standards, the Limiting Line of Line Equipment (LLE) would necessitate the placement of PSDs sufficiently distant from the train doors to create gaps that would have to be addressed by entrapment prevention measures.
### [p499_c0014] Platform Edge Condition
[p499_c0015] The platform edges were reconstructed within the past thirty years. From our limited visual inspection and our knowledge of repair strategies used in station rehabilitations over the last thirty years, structural work would at a minimum be required for the installation of an APG system.
[p499_c0016] _Page 45 of 81 March 29, 2019_

[p500_c0006] _Figure 1 – Overall Station Plan 103rd Street Station_

### [p501_c0004] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (103 rd Street Station)

## Page 502

### [p502_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p502_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p502_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (103 rd Street Station)
### [p502_c0004] Platform obstructions within 5' of edge:
- [p502_c0005] • None
### [p502_c0006] Lighting:
[p502_c0007] Existing lighting: Throughout both platforms there are linear florescent fixtures mounted parallel to the platform edge. Depending on the specific APG/PSD design used, there could be no or minimal alterations to the existing lighting configuration
### [p502_c0008] Power:
[p502_c0009] This station has adequate electrical capacity to support the implementation of an APG/PSD system. An analysis of electrical reserve service could not be performed due to inaccessibility during survey. We do not consider a lack of adequate existing power to be a determining factor of future feasibility. If in the future, a PSD/APG project is to be implemented at this station, and it is determined at that time that an upgrade in power service to the station is required, it would simply mean an increased cost to the project. Below in Table 1 see the Power Capacity Analysis for this station.
[p502_c0010] _Station Power Capacity Analysis (Normal service)_
[p502_c0011]

```text
Station Name 103rd Street Peak Demand Load from ConEd Report, (kW) 26.8 Apparent Power (kVA) 33.5 Station Peak Demand Load, Max Current, (A) 93.1 Maximum Amount of Doors 60.0 PSD Total Load Including All Miscellaneous Loads, (A) 158.1 Total Load (Station Peak + PSD), (A) 251.2 Station Service Power Capacity, (Main SB or SG Rating), (A) 400.0 Service Spare Capacity, (A) 148.9 Is Electrical Service Adequate? Yes Notes Service rating of 400A is based on field observations. Station has only (1) meter readings for Normal service. The station Reserve meter was NOT accessible during field survey. (No meter in Reserve EDR) 1 Line diagram provided is not current.
```
[p502_c0012] _Table 1. Normal Service Power Capacity Analysis_
[p502_c0013] _Page 48 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 503

### [p503_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p503_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p503_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations
[p503_c0004] (103 rd Street Station)
### [p503_c0005] Historic Restrictions:
[p503_c0006] None
### [p503_c0007] Rough Order-of-Magnitude Cost Estimate:
[p503_c0008] The rough order-of-magnitude (ROM) cost estimate is based on a summary of anticipated costs developed through a review of field conditions, analysis of space constraints and existing documentation. It is not based upon an actual conceptual design. The basis of estimate assumptions are listed in the attached ROM estimate. The total cost for this station is estimated to be $27.6M to install APGs and $34.5M to install PSDs (See Appendix E)
[p503_c0009] _Page 49 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 504

### [p504_c0001] Table of Contents

### [p504_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4' Line Stations (96 th Street Station)
### [p504_c0004] 1.35 – MR 396 | 96 th Street
[p504_c0005] Summary: 96 th Street Station is not feasible for both APGs and PSDs as their implementation would result in non-compliant egress conditions. In the implementation of a platform edge barrier, the 5'-11" minimum corridor requirement for evacuation would not be met at the north end of the southbound platform as the existing width is 5'-0" (see figure 1).
### [p504_c0006] Description
[p504_c0007] 96 th Street Station is a below-grade station with two straight side platforms. The platform structures are cast-in-place concrete. The width of the platforms ranges from 5'-0" to 12'-10".
[p504_c0008] The platform width at the end of the southbound platform is 5'-0" or 60". Our station egress analysis (attached as Appendix C) finds that 5'-11" is a minimum side platform width which will not impede egress with an installed PSD system. See figure 1 for reference.
[p504_c0009] _Figure 1 – Non-Compliant egress condition 96 th Street Station_
[p504_c0010] _Page 50 of 81 March 29, 2019_

## Page 505

### [p505_c0001] Table of Contents
### [p505_c0002] NYC Transit Platform Screen Doors Pilot Project –Contract C-32516
### [p505_c0003] Tier 2-3 Report on Feasibility of Platform Edge Barriers for '4'
